name: DNSSEC Validation and Misconfiguration
description: Detects DNSSEC deployment issues including missing signing, expired RRSIG signatures, weak algorithms, incorrect NSEC/NSEC3 configuration, and broken chain of trust. Per NIST SP 800-81r3 Section 3.8, ECDSA P-256/P-384 and Ed25519/Ed448 are preferred over RSA, RRSIG validity should be 5-7 days, and NSEC is preferred over NSEC3.
author: AC3 ScanForge

values: No DS records in parent zone

remediation: Enable DNSSEC signing on all authoritative zones. Use ECDSA P-256 (Algorithm 13) or Ed25519 (Algorithm 15) instead of RSA. Set RRSIG validity periods to 5-7 days to limit the window of compromise. Use NSEC instead of NSEC3 unless organizational policy requires zone walking protection. If NSEC3 is required, set iteration count to 0 per RFC 9276. Ensure DS records are properly published in the parent zone. Plan for post-quantum algorithm migration when specifications become available.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: Lack of DNSSEC enables DNS cache poisoning and man-in-the-middle attacks. Nation-state actors have exploited missing DNSSEC to redirect DNS queries and intercept credentials in targeted campaigns.

complianceMapping.controlTitle: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
